translations.descricao: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (DM2) is a chronic metabolic disease characterized by hyperglycemia resulting from defects in insulin secretion and/or insulin action. It represents the most prevalent form of diabetes, affecting millions globally and being one of the main causes of morbidity and mortality. In the Brazilian context, DM2 is a public health priority, with integrated protocols for prevention, diagnosis, and treatment.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening recommended for adults with overweight (BMI ≥25 kg/m²) and risk factors, or ≥35 years, aiming for early detection and prevention of complications.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults ≥35 years; individuals <35 years with BMI ≥25 kg/m², hypertension, dyslipidemia, family history of DM2 or pregnant women at risk.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The American Diabetes Association (ADA 2024) and the Brazilian Diabetes Society (SBD 2023-2024) recommend screening for adults ≥35 years with overweight or risk factors, emphasizing a personalized approach.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Globally, it affects about 10.5% of adults (537 million in 2021), projected to 783 million by 2045. In Brazil, prevalence of 7.4% in adults (approximately 19 million).
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global incidence of 5.9 million new cases annually. In Brazil, about 1.5 million new cases per year.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Causes 6.7 million global deaths in 2021 (9th leading cause). In Brazil, responsible for 5.3% of deaths from chronic diseases.
